PricingDocsGitHub

Free Tool

Free Terms of Service Generator for Developers

Stop copying generic terms of service templates. Codepliant scans your codebase to understand what your application does — user accounts, payments, file uploads, API access — and generates terms that actually match your product.

Why generate ToS from code?

Generic terms of service templates are written for generic applications. They include clauses for features you don't have and miss features you do. If your app handles payments but your ToS doesn't mention refund policies, you have a problem.

Codepliant detects whether your app has user authentication, payment processing (Stripe, PayPal), file uploads, user-generated content, real-time features, API endpoints, and more. The generated terms of service include relevant sections and omit irrelevant ones.

Features detected for ToS generation

User registration & accounts
Payment processing & billing
File uploads & storage
User-generated content
API access & rate limits
Real-time features (WebSocket)
Email & notifications
Third-party integrations
AI-powered features
Data export capabilities

Generate your terms of service

Free, open source, no account required.

npx codepliant go

Frequently asked questions

How does Codepliant generate terms of service?

Codepliant scans your codebase for features like user authentication, payment processing, file uploads, user-generated content, and API integrations. It uses this information to generate terms of service that accurately describe what your application does and the legal terms under which users access it.

What sections are included in the generated ToS?

The generated terms of service include: acceptance of terms, description of service, user accounts, prohibited conduct, intellectual property, payment terms (if applicable), limitation of liability, termination, governing law, and dispute resolution. Sections are included or excluded based on what your code actually does.

Can I customize the generated terms?

Yes. The generated document is yours to modify. You can add your company name, jurisdiction, specific clauses, and any additional terms. We recommend having a lawyer review the final version.

Is this free?

Yes. The CLI is free and open source. Generate terms of service in Markdown at no cost. Pro plan ($29/mo) adds HTML and PDF output with custom branding.
